commit.message:
feat(logging): add Loki + Promtail for log aggregation (step 09)

- Loki StatefulSet on pi4controller, single-process mode, 3Gi Longhorn PVC
  - TSDB v13 schema, 7-day retention via compactor
  - ClusterIP Service at loki:3100 for Promtail + Grafana
- Promtail DaemonSet on all 5 nodes
  - RBAC: ServiceAccount + ClusterRole (get/list/watch pods/nodes)
  - Scrapes /var/log/pods via kubernetes_sd_configs (pod role)
  - Tolerations for control-plane taint
- Grafana datasource ConfigMap updated to include Loki (http://loki:3100)
- ArgoCD Application CRDs added for loki and promtail (auto-sync from main)
- bootstrap/docs/09-loki-promtail.md with architecture, verification steps,
  LogQL query examples, and troubleshooting guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(monitoring): increase prometheus memory limit to fix OOMKill crash loop

Prometheus was being OOMKilled on every startup (exit code 137) while
replaying 848 accumulated WAL segments. The 256Mi limit was too low for
WAL replay on startup; the Pi 4 controller has ~1.4GB free headroom.

Increased limit 256Mi -> 512Mi and request 64Mi -> 256Mi.
Once Prometheus stays up long enough to compact the WAL, memory usage
will drop back to normal levels.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rename sandbox setting to chat.agent.sandbox (#303421) (#305846)

* Rename sandbox setting to chat.agent.sandbox (#303421)

Rename the top-level sandbox setting from `chat.tools.terminal.sandbox.enabled`
to `chat.agent.sandbox` to reflect that sandboxing is a general agent concept,
not terminal-specific.

- Update setting ID value to `chat.agent.sandbox`
- Update description to be more general
- Deprecate old `chat.tools.terminal.sandbox.enabled` setting
- Update telemetry event name

Fixes #303421

commit.message:
feat(theming): Full Skins & Themes Implementation (#369) (#528)

* feat(theming): Full Skins & Themes implementation (#369)

Wave 1 - Core Theme Fidelity:
- ThemeMode enum (StyleSheetTheme/Theme) with dual-mode ApplyThemeSkin
- Sub-component style theming (SubStyles on ControlSkin, SkinBuilder.SubStyle())
- 5 data controls override ApplyThemeSkin: GridView, DetailsView, FormView, DataGrid, DataList
- Container-level EnableTheming propagation via ancestor chain walk
- Runtime theme switching via ThemeProvider Mode parameter
- Fix generic type name lookup (GridView1 -> GridView) for theme skin matching

Wave 2 - Migration Accelerators:
- .skin file parser (SkinFileParser) - reads Web Forms .skin files into ThemeConfiguration
- JSON theme format (JsonThemeLoader) - load/save themes as JSON with custom converters
- CSS file bundling - ThemeProvider renders <link> elements via HeadContent

Wave 3 - Diagnostics:
- ThemeDiagnostics with validation rules for unknown controls, sub-styles, empty skins
- Runtime SkinID mismatch logging in BaseWebFormsComponent

Tests: 120 theming tests (72 Wave 1 + 48 Wave 2), 2685 total tests passing
Docs: themes-and-skins.md with migration guide, API reference, quick start

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(squad): log skins & themes session

Session: 2026-03-28T14-36-00Z-skins-themes-full
Requested by: Squad Coordinator

Changes:
- Orchestration logs for Cyclops, Rogue, Beast, Bishop
- Session log summarizing Wave 1 + Wave 2 implementation
- Merged 7 decision inbox files into decisions.md
- Deduplication: No exact duplicates found

WI-1 through WI-11 and bug fixes documented.
All 127 tests passing, build clean.

* feat(theming): Auto-discover themes in AddBlazorWebFormsComponents (#369)

- BlazorWebFormsComponentsOptions gains ThemesPath and ThemeMode properties
- AddBlazorWebFormsComponents() registers ThemeConfiguration singleton that
  auto-discovers .skin and .css files from wwwroot/App_Themes/ at resolution time
- ThemeProvider falls back to DI-registered ThemeConfiguration when no explicit
  Theme parameter is set (explicit always wins)
- Added theme-migration SKILL.md for Copilot/agent migration guidance

Migration is now: copy App_Themes/ to wwwroot/App_Themes/ — zero extra lines needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(squad): log theme auto-discovery session

Session: 2026-03-28-theme-auto-discovery
Requested by: Scribe

Changes:
- Logged orchestration entries for Cyclops and Bishop
- Logged session summary for theme auto-discovery work
- Merged decisions from inbox to decisions.md
- Cleared decision inbox

* feat(theming): Add ThemeMode and sub-style sample demos with acceptance tests (#369)

- Section 7: ThemeMode StyleSheetTheme vs Theme side-by-side comparison
- Section 8: GridView sub-styles (HeaderStyle, AlternatingRowStyle, FooterStyle)
- 2 new Playwright acceptance tests for both new sections
- 2,685 unit tests passing, build clean

commit.message:
feat: Ask Before Working Preflight-Check (Superpowers #6)

Pflicht-Checkliste vor Code-Schreiben: Requirements verstanden?
Mehrdeutigkeiten? Dateien bekannt? File-Structure-Mapping erstellt?
Architekturentscheidungen gelesen?

Verbindet sich mit Escalation-Grade (NEEDS_CONTEXT) und
File-Structure-Mapping. Anti-Patterns dokumentiert.

Quelle: obra/superpowers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Spec-Versionierung in docs/specs/ (Superpowers #5)

Konvention für versionierte Feature-Spezifikationen:
- Format: docs/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-{feature-slug}.md
- Git-committed für Traceability
- README mit Naming-Konvention, Regeln und Template
- Integriert File-Structure-Map ins Spec-Template

Quelle: obra/superpowers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: VERIFY-before-RESPOND Checklist für Reviews (Superpowers #4)

Stark's Charter um strukturiertes Review-Protokoll erweitert:
READ → UNDERSTAND → VERIFY → EVALUATE → RESPOND → IMPLEMENT.
Anti-Pattern gegen blinde Zustimmung. Pushback-Kategorien für
begründete Ablehnung: Breaking Change, Context Gap, YAGNI,
Tech Mismatch, Architecture Conflict.

Quelle: obra/superpowers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Two-Pass Code Review Ceremony (Superpowers #3)

Zwei separate Review-Durchläufe nach jeder Implementierung:
1. Spec Review — Vollständigkeit, Korrektheit, fehlende Features
2. Code Quality Review — Best Practices, Tests, Performance, Sicherheit

Separate Dispatches, nicht ein kombinierter Pass. Design Review
um File-Structure-Mapping als Pflicht-Input erweitert.

Quelle: obra/superpowers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: File-Structure-Mapping vor Task-Dekomposition (Superpowers #2)

Vor jeder Task-Zerlegung muss ein File-Structure-Mapping erstellt werden:
welche Dateien werden erstellt, modifiziert oder gelöscht. Verhindert
vergessene Abhängigkeiten während der Implementierung.

Quelle: obra/superpowers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Escalation-Grade für Agenten (Superpowers #1)

Agenten müssen ihren Abschlussstatus mit DONE, DONE_WITH_CONCERNS,
NEEDS_CONTEXT oder BLOCKED melden. Jeder Grade hat definierte
Pflichtangaben für strukturierte Kommunikation.

Quelle: obra/superpowers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- add ranked execution plan from outreach through scale
- keep positioning tied to validated capabilities and proof needs

commit.message:
feat: AI-Entwicklungsstandards aus obra/superpowers integrieren

Drei neue Squad-Skills und Copilot-Instructions aus dem superpowers-Repo
(https://github.com/obra/superpowers, MIT-Lizenz) adaptiert fuer unseren
Flutter/Dart + ASP.NET Core Stack:

- .github/copilot-instructions.md: Projekt-weite Copilot-Anweisungen mit
  TDD, Code-Standards (Dart + C#), Testing-Anti-Patterns, Debugging,
  Commit-Konventionen und Projektstruktur
- .squad/skills/test-driven-development/: TDD Red-Green-Refactor mit
  Flutter/xUnit-Beispielen + Testing-Anti-Patterns-Referenz
- .squad/skills/systematic-debugging/: 4-Phasen Root-Cause-Analyse mit
  Defense-in-Depth Validierung, angepasst fuer Flutter<->API<->DB Stack
- .squad/skills/verification-before-completion/: Pflicht zur Verifikation
  vor jeder Fertigmeldung (flutter test, dotnet test, flutter analyze)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement GatherBlockQuantized operator (#673)

* feat: implement GatherBlockQuantized operator

Add support for the com.microsoft::GatherBlockQuantized contrib operator
which combines gather and block dequantization in a single operation.

Supports:
- Packed data (UINT8 containing 4-bit values) and native sub-byte types (INT4, UINT4)
- 4-bit and 8-bit quantization
- Optional zero_points (packed or unpacked)
- Configurable gather_axis and quantize_axis
- Default zero_point of 2^(bits-1) for unsigned data types

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/emmtrix/emx-onnx-cgen/sessions/d7122d68-6c9f-4642-8adf-e4e10814bcea

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update golden references for GatherBlockQuantized

Update expected_errors, ONNX_ERRORS.md, ONNX_SUPPORT.md and SUPPORT_OPS.md
to reflect the new GatherBlockQuantized operator support.
All 6682 tests pass (1 pre-existing failure in test_cli_verify_reduce_model).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/emmtrix/emx-onnx-cgen/sessions/d7122d68-6c9f-4642-8adf-e4e10814bcea

commit.message:
fix: auto-kill old backend before build + set cwd to repo root

- Stop-ExistingBackend kills any running Sheetstorm.Api processes
  before dotnet build to prevent DLL lock errors (MSB3026)
- Set-Location to repo root so the script works from any subdirectory

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(auth): align Flutter JSON keys with backend camelCase format

The backend (ASP.NET Core) returns camelCase JSON field names by default
(accessToken, refreshToken, tokenType, expiresIn), but the Flutter
AuthTokens.fromJson was parsing snake_case keys (access_token, etc.).
This caused a null TypeError on json['access_token'] as String, which
was caught by the generic catch-all handler, surfacing as
'Ein unbekannter Fehler ist aufgetreten' on login.

Changes:
- AuthTokens.fromJson: snake_case keys -> camelCase keys
- auth_service.dart refresh request body: refresh_token -> refreshToken
- auth_notifier.dart login catch-all: log actual error for debuggability

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: update German-to-English renames across codebase

- Rename providers: configNotifierProvider->configProvider,
  performanceModeNotifierProvider->performanceModeProvider,
  spielmodusSettingsProvider->performanceModeSettingsProvider,
  mitgliederProvider->membersProvider, registerProvider->sectionProvider
- Rename types: Stimme->Voice, Mitglied->Member, Register->Section
- Rename fields: stimme->voice, stufe->level, privat->isPrivate,
  stimme->isVoice, orchester->isOrchestra
- Fix imports in app_router.dart
- Fix _PolicyTile.key conflict with Widget.key
- Fix Color import in design_tokens_test.dart
- Regenerate .g.dart files via build_runner

repository.name: pt-arche-kubernetes-opa-gatekeeper
repository.description: OpenTofu example module for Open Policy Agent Gatekeeper on Google Kubernetes Engine.

commit.message:
Add invoice review screen before import

- New InvoiceReviewScreen lets user select/deselect products before
  importing; products already in the DB are pre-deselected and shown
  with an 'Already in list' badge
- New InvoiceReviewViewModel (activity-scoped) parses PDFs, tracks
  checkbox state, and executes the import for selected products only
- ImportScreen now navigates to the review screen instead of importing
  directly; supports multiple PDFs selected at once
- 3-dot menu on review screen: Select All / Deselect All
- Result (X added, Y skipped) shown inline after import with Done button

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
